mechanism_narrative: Sustained low federal funds rates compress equity discount rates and lower corporate borrowing costs, directly supporting higher S&P 500 valuations and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX delivers negative 20-day returns in over 60% of periods when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 8.0 percent signals a structural improvement in labor market conditions, reducing recession risk premiums and triggering institutional portfolio rebalancing into equities.
failure_condition: UNRATE crosses below 8.0 but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain negative in more than 55% of observed instances, or the signal loses predictive power during labor market normalization.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and stable consumer price inflation indicates sustainable aggregate demand growth without triggering aggressive central bank tightening, supporting equity earnings revisions.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth coincides with CPIAUCSL acceleration above 2.5% annually, causing rate hike expectations to dominate and produce negative 60-day SPX returns despite strong spending data.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly reduces corporate profit margins and triggers immediate short-term equity selling pressure.
failure_condition: INDPRO registers consecutive monthly declines but S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in over 65% of cases due to liquidity injections or sector rotation away from industrials.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 700 thousand units signals a definitive turnaround in residential construction, driving upstream supply chain revenues and consumer wealth effects that lift broad equity markets.
failure_condition: HOUST crosses the 700.0 threshold but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered months, indicating decoupling between housing activity and equity valuations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm broad-based employment growth, lowering tail-risk discount rates and supporting longer-term equity appreciation.
failure_condition: PAYEMS rises while UNRATE falls, yet S&P 500 delivers negative 60-day returns in over 60% of observations due to external geopolitical shocks or valuation compression overriding labor improvements.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation while policy rates remain anchored near zero forces markets to price in future monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL accelerates past the threshold but S&P 500 rises over 20 days as markets interpret price increases as demand-driven growth rather than supply shocks, neutralizing rate hike fears.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarterly GDP growth momentum translates into upward corporate revenue revisions, with expanding economic output directly driving short-term equity buying pressure and higher forward returns.
failure_condition: GDPC1 shows positive sequential growth but S&P 500 declines over 5 trading days in more than 55% of release periods due to liquidity drains or profit-taking overriding macro fundamentals.
